query: WEAVER complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Our Reviews | Weaver Loveless Law
results.content:
# Reviews

We engaged their services as an out-of-state property owner who ran into some difficulties with title after the fact. Needless to say we came into our initial communication with a fair level of frustration. Right from the start we felt confident and at ease and even enjoyed our interactions. The efficiency and quality of the work performed was excellent. In fact, I have come to expect much less from service providers of all types over the last 10 years. Our experience with this firm not only surprised me but actually made me feel very good on multiple levels, not the least of which is that I finally had confidence that my best interest was in fact being served West integrity and competency. The supplies to all the people we came in contact with within the firm. [...] We hired Mr. Weaver and his firm to assist us in purchasing a home in Haines City. We live in Michigan and the owner of the home was living in Wisconsin. We weren't sure how the process was going to work, but Melissa handled everything in a very professional and friendly manner. There were a few bumps, but Melissa smoothed them out by even working past her normal business hours to meet our needs. Highly recommend this firm! [...] James Weaver and his firm handled the closing on a building I owned in downtown Lake Wales. Even with the hurdles that usually come with a closing, this closing was handled with great professionalism. Melissa kept everyone abreast of what was going on and when we thought we hit a brick wall, Mr. Weaver stepped in and had the problem resolved very quickly. I highly recommend this firm. Thanks to all for a job well done!!!

results.title: REVIEW: Bitter, Sweet: How to Heal Yourself When Your Family Is Broken by Stephanie Weaver | Hippocampus Magazine
results.content: I never thought I’d say a book about CSA and family dysfunction was compulsively readable, but it was. Weaver’s writing is approachable, and secure in the telling. Most of the events that Weaver describes happened over 30 years ago; the most recent ones are nearly a decade old. Meaning that Weaver has clearly had time to process and analyze what has happened to her without drawing directly from the emotion itself. Write from the scar, not the wound is how the popular adage goes, and Weaver puts that on full display here. [...] At the outset, Weaver makes it clear that this is her personal journey. Her story is neatly contrasted with that of her sister, who remained estranged from their parents until their deaths. Weaver does not judge her sister, nor anyone who decides to stay estranged from family members. She is simply speaking to her experience, and recounts the tools she used to reestablish contact once she realized that was what she wanted. Weaver is suggestive without being prescriptive: she describes the therapies and alternative modalities she used without false promises that they will work for everyone. [...] This book can be for anyone who has dealt with family estrangement, and has an interest in reopening those lines of communication, who felt as Weaver did that ‘something was missing.’ I would recommend reading trigger warnings, as the book does contain some graphic descriptions, especially when Weaver describes the abuse she and her siblings endured. It is not self-help per se, but an offering of how one person managed to regain contact. The book can serve as a road map, or merely an opportunity for reflection. While unusual in the self-help space, the subtitle implies the book is more self-help than it actually is.

results.title: Peer Review | Weaver
results.content:
System Reviews determine whether or not your firm’s system of quality control conforms to the professional standards, including Statements on Quality Control Standards issued by the American Institute of CPAs (AICPA) and whether such a system is appropriately designed. These reviews cover areas such as whether or not management is meeting its responsibilities for quality within your firm (the “tone at the top”); ethical requirements such as independence, integrity and objectivity; acceptance and continuance of client relationships and specific engagements; and human resources systems, engagement performance and monitoring. [...] Weaver

# Peer Review

If your organization is an accounting firm, consider having Weaver provide your peer review. We perform both system and engagement reviews.

## Trending Insights & Resources

## Weaver can perform two types of peer reviews for your firm: [...] Engagement Reviews are available to firms that do not perform audits or attestation engagements. The objective of an Engagement Review is to evaluate whether engagements (and certain engagement documentation) submitted for review are performed and reported upon in conformity with applicable professional standards in all material respects.

results.content:
★★★★★

Thomas Helber

"Great firm to work with. No matter how big or small your case is, they treat everyone the same. They work hard to deliver positive results. Highly recommend this firm. I’ll use these folks for any legal matters that I have."

★★★★★

Justin Lamberth

"First time using a lawyer and have no regret in my decision. Very professional and attentive. Definitely knows their stuff. I would highly recommend. Will definitely be my lifetime lawyer."

★★★★★

Jeremy Konkler

"Weaver Law was extremely professional throughout my legal dispute. They were able to break down my options and answered all my questions along the way. I would gladly reach out again if I ever need their services and would recommend them to all!"

★★★★★

O.zzie Gonzalez [...] ★★★★★

Steve Williams

"I had a legal issue come up early this year and decided to hire Weaver Law Offices to represent me in my legal matter. They were professional and responsive every step of the way. I had never dealt with any kind of legal issue in my life and I was very overwhelmed and intimidated with the whole situation. However, the staff and the Attorneys that worked alongside me in my case made me feel confident and at ease throughout the entire process. I highly recommend this firm if you are looking for Attorneys who genuinely care about your case and give you both legal guidance and professional representation from beginning to end!"

★★★★★

Matthew Roberts [...] Write A Review

"Highly recommend!"

★★★★★

"It had been 20 years since we had touched our Last Will and we needed to upgrade it with changes that have come about in that time. We needed a credible lawyer and with an office nearby. Weaver Law office was what we checked out and we were quite satisfied with the work they had done. For us their expertise and guidance developing our documents of our Last Will, our Power of Attorneys, and Trust document for handling our expressed needs was was very professional and handled in a reasonable amount of time. We were well satisfied with the service they extended to us."
